label: Cisternhold Quarters
description: Cisternhold Quarters is a compact residence built into the lee of Seamglass Meridian’s causeway foundations, where faultlight seam-lines remain steady enough to permit lens-calibrated living spaces. Faceted interior walls are clad in dust-slick vitrified tile, scored with grounded channels that collect electrostatic glass fallouts into a sealed, undersill cistern. Copper-white twilight enters through narrow, mirrored light slits that split the city’s calibrated bands into fixed measures for local timekeeping. The plan centers on a low hearthstone of fused silicate and a ring of recessed alcoves designed to keep surfaces clear of drifting glass dust.
